build.note: Blended group WACC: subscription segment 9.0% (dominant, 91.8% of EV) and advertising segment 10.5% (8.2% of EV) blend to ~9.12%; rounded to 9.0%. Beta 1.0 reflects Netflix's status as a pure-play subscription + ads platform — correlation to tech cycle (high) offset by consumer-defensive streaming subscription quality (low). Risk-free 4.2% (10-yr UST as of Jun 2026). ERP 5.5% (Damodaran estimate).

target_12m.multiple_rationale: 28x P/E on FY2027E $3.60 EPS ($3.00 FY2026E growing 20% as ad EBIT approaches $1B and subscription EBIT margin expands to 33%+). Justified multiple range: subscription alone at 22x EBIT = $87.85/sh base (27x current P/E); advertising at scale adds re-rate potential. 28x is the multiple the market will pay once $3B FY2026 ad revenue is in results and the trajectory toward $5B+ is visible. Peers: Amazon at 45x FY2027E EPS, Apple at 30x — Netflix at 28x is the conservative end of what a subscription+growing-ads platform commands. NOT the consensus 33-35x (implies full $9B ad scenario), NOT the current 27x (prices zero advertising premium). The re-rate from 27x to 28x is minimal and requires only that the $3B FY2026 ad guidance is delivered — contracted by management.
